title: Podman stops accepting terminal input
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Podman seems to intermittently corrupt the terminal somehow while running containers. Once this happens, any new containers will not accept any input or the detach keys. The only way to regain control is by stopping the container from another terminal. When the container exits, all the keys typed into it are dumped to the shell which then causes "command not found" or other errors depending on which keys had been pressed. This issue continues to happen for any new containers started in the same terminal window. Resetting the terminal doesn't fix it, but it can be fixed either by starting a new subshell or by closing the terminal window and opening a new one.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start a container attached to the terminal.

2. Use the container some and then exit it

3. Repeat steps 1-2 an indeterminate number of times. At some point a newly started container will not accept any input.

4. Stop the container from another terminal (`podman stop -l`)

5. Run any container in the original terminal window (Ex: `podman run --rm -ti ubuntu`) and see it still will not accept any input.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I have run across this multiple times while developing and testing various containerized apps. I believe it's happened in both root and rootless mode, but I mostly use rootless. Once the issue is triggered neither mode works in that terminal window as tested with:
`podman run --rm -ti ubuntu` and `sudo podman run --rm -ti ubuntu`.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

```
podman/unknown,now 1.8.0~7 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Ubuntu 18.04

comments.body:
I just ran across something that might help. After I had a container get stuck and stop accepting input, I stopped it in another terminal per usual as above. However, this time I tried running another podman command (that wasn't podman run), and this was the result:
```
$ podman container prune
WARNING! This will remove all stopped containers.
Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] Error: error reading input: read /dev/stdin: resource temporarily unavailable
$
```
It wrote the prompt out, but never gave me a chance to pick yes or no, it immediately failed when it tried to read stdin. So that gives some more idea of how stdin is getting corrupted!

I just ran `sh` to clear the error so my terminal was usable again and I got this potentially relevant message:
```
user@host$ sh
$ sh: turning off NDELAY mode

```

Can you try clearing terminal configuration with `reset` to see if that resolves it? I'm wondering if some of the changes we make for terminal attach (setting raw mode, among a few other things) might be being set, but not cleared.
Resetting the terminal was the first thing I tried, sadly it doesn't help.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@computator @mheon Does this still happen with the latest versions of podman and conmon?
I haven't had it happen yet, but I haven't been using containers much in the last week or two and may just not have triggered it yet. I will update this if I run into it again.
Ok reopen if it happens again.
